competencies.name: Data Pipeline Design
competencies.description: Designs reliable, scalable, and maintainable data pipelines; understands batch vs. streaming tradeoffs; handles failures gracefully with idempotency and retries.
positive_indicators: distinguishes batch and streaming with appropriate use cases
discusses idempotency and how to achieve it
handles late-arriving data and out-of-order events
designs for failure with retries, dead-letter queues, and alerting
thinks about pipeline observability from the start
negative_indicators: cannot explain when to use streaming vs. batch
pipeline design has no error handling or retry logic
does not consider late data or partial failures
has not thought about monitoring pipeline health

competencies.name: Data Modeling & Schema Design
competencies.description: Designs schemas that serve analytical and operational needs; understands normalization, star/snowflake schemas, and partitioning strategies.
positive_indicators: explains when to denormalize for analytical performance
designs partitioning strategy based on query patterns
discusses slowly changing dimensions and history tracking
considers schema evolution and backward compatibility
negative_indicators: applies OLTP normalization rules to analytical workloads
does not consider partitioning or clustering
ignores how the schema will evolve over time
cannot explain the difference between star and snowflake schemas

competencies.name: Performance & Scalability
competencies.description: Optimizes queries and pipelines for scale; understands distributed computing tradeoffs; identifies bottlenecks and resolves them.
positive_indicators: explains query plan analysis and how to interpret it
identifies data skew as a common distributed bottleneck
discusses caching, materialization, and pre-aggregation strategies
benchmarks before optimizing rather than guessing
negative_indicators: optimizes without measuring first
cannot explain what a shuffle is or why it is expensive
does not consider data distribution in distributed jobs
cannot read or interpret a query execution plan

competencies.name: Data Quality & Reliability
competencies.description: Implements data validation; detects anomalies and schema drift; ensures SLAs on data freshness and completeness; treats data quality as a first-class concern.
positive_indicators: defines data quality checks at ingestion, transformation, and serving
discusses how to detect and alert on schema changes
handles null, duplicate, and out-of-range values explicitly
sets freshness SLAs and monitors against them
negative_indicators: treats data quality as a downstream consumer's problem
has no strategy for detecting silent data failures
no mention of null handling or deduplication
cannot describe how they would set a data freshness SLA

competencies.name: Tool & Technology Judgment
competencies.description: Selects tools appropriate to the problem scale and team context; understands tradeoffs between Spark, Flink, dbt, Airflow, Kafka, and cloud-native alternatives.
positive_indicators: selects tools based on the problem, not familiarity
explains what a tool is bad at, not just good at
mentions operational overhead as a factor in tool selection
can name the key tradeoff between two competing tools
negative_indicators: recommends only tools they have used before
cannot articulate weaknesses of their preferred tools
ignores operational cost and maintenance burden
cannot compare two tools in the same category

competencies.name: Stakeholder Collaboration
competencies.description: Works with analysts, scientists, and PMs to understand data needs; translates business requirements into data models; communicates data limitations clearly.
positive_indicators: describes how they gather data requirements from non-engineers
communicates data limitations and caveats to downstream users
proactively flags when a data request is ambiguous
negative_indicators: builds what was asked without validating the underlying need
does not communicate data quality issues to consumers
treats data modeling as purely a technical decision
